descript.abstract: According to ARS 45-402(34) "Subbasin" means an area which, as nearly as known facts permit as determined by the director pursuant to this chapter, may be designated so as to enclose a relatively hydrologically distinct body of groundwater within a groundwater basin, which shall be described horizontally by surface description.
descript.purpose: This feature contains groundwater Sub-basin boundaries and provide base information for use in GIS systems to aid in assessment for a variety of planning and analysis purposes and to provide a geographic view with corresponding data. These data do not represent a legal record
descript.supplinf:
From: Kenneth C. Slowinski Sent: Wednesday, December 10, 2008 11:13 AM To: E. Frank Corkhill Cc: W. Patrick Schiffer Subject: RE: Question
In the July 20, 1982 order designating groundwater basins outside of the four AMAs, the director designated 47 basins, 10 of which were divided into a total of 34 sub-basins. That order was not final because it was subject to rehearing. Rehearings were held, and a final order was issued on June 21, 1984. In the final 1984 order, the director designated 46 basins outside of the four AMAs, 9 of which were divided into a total of 27 sub-basins. In the 1984 order, the director combined the San Francisco River and Eagle Creek basins into one basin - the Morenci basin. There also were changes to several of the sub-basins. All the changes between the 1982 order and the 1984 order are described in the notebook entitled "Basin and Sub-basin Designations, June 1984."
There were no changes to the Verde River basin or the sub-basins within that basin between the 1982 order and the 1984 order. However, in the 1982 order, there are findings that explain that the Verde River basin was originally proposed as three separate basins: the Mount Floyd basin, the Verde Valley basin and the Verde River basin. The three proposed basins were ultimately combined into one basin in the 1982 order (the Verde River basin), and the basin was divided into three sub-basins: the Big Chino sub-basin, the Verde Valley sub-basin and the Verde Canyon sub-basins. The 1982 findings explain why the three proposed basins were combined into one basin and also explain that the eastern boundary of the basin was changed from the approximate boundary of the C aquifer system to the Verde River surface watershed divide. If you want to see the explanations from the 1982 order, they are in the book entitled "Groundwater Basin and Sub-basin Designations, ADWR, July 1982 (pages 32-33).

idinfo.accconst: Access is granted to public agencies. The Department makes no warranties related to the accuracy of the data and users are required to determine suitability of use for any particular purpose.
idinfo.useconst: Not for use at scales greater than 1:100000.
idinfo.native: Microsoft Windows XP Version 5.1 (Build 2600) Service Pack 3; ESRI ArcCatalog 9.3.0.1770

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Active Management Codes; B = Prescott, C = Phoenix, D = Pinal, E = Tucson, H = Santa Cruz

cntorgp.cntper: REQUIRED: The person responsible for the metadata information.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standards for Digital Geospatial Metadata

lineage.Process: FeatureClassToFeatureClass "Database Connections\Connection to adwrsde.azwater.gov.sde\LIB.Groundwaterbasin_Surfacewatershed\LIB.groundwatersubbasin_dwr" "Database Connections\Connection to adwrsde.azwater.gov.sde\LIB.Hydrology" groundwater_subbasin_dwr # "AMA_CODE 'AMA_CODE' true true false 1 Text 0 0 ,First,#,Database Connections\Connection to adwrsde.azwater.gov.sde\LIB.Groundwaterbasin_Surfacewatershed\LIB.groundwatersubbasin_dwr,AMA_CODE,-1,-1;SUBBASIN_CODE 'SUBBASIN_CODE' true true false 2 Text 0 0 ,First,#,Database Connections\Connection to adwrsde.azwater.gov.sde\LIB.Groundwaterbasin_Surfacewatershed\LIB.groundwatersubbasin_dwr,SUBBASIN_CODE,-1,-1;SUBBASIN_NAME 'SUBBASIN_NAME' true true false 40 Text 0 0 ,First,#,Database Connections\Connection to adwrsde.azwater.gov.sde\LIB.Groundwaterbasin_Surfacewatershed\LIB.groundwatersubbasin_dwr,SUBBASIN_NAME,-1,-1;NAME_ABBR 'NAME_ABBR' true true false 3 Text 0 0 ,First,#,Database Connections\Connection to adwrsde.azwater.gov.sde\LIB.Groundwaterbasin_Surfacewatershed\LIB.groundwatersubbasin_dwr,NAME_ABBR,-1,-1;SHAPE_AREA 'SHAPE_AREA' false false true 0 Double 0 0 ,First,#,Database Connections\Connection to adwrsde.azwater.gov.sde\LIB.Groundwaterbasin_Surfacewatershed\LIB.groundwatersubbasin_dwr,SHAPE.AREA,-1,-1;SHAPE_LEN 'SHAPE_LEN' false false true 0 Double 0 0 ,First,#,Database Connections\Connection to adwrsde.azwater.gov.sde\LIB.Groundwaterbasin_Surfacewatershed\LIB.groundwatersubbasin_dwr,SHAPE.LEN,-1,-1" STATIC "Database Connections\Connection to adwrsde.azwater.gov.sde\LIB.Hydrology\LIB.groundwater_subbasin_dwr"

FeatureClassToFeatureClass "Database Connections\adwrsde_9.3_login.sde\LIB.Hydrological\LIB.groundwater_subbasin_dwr" U:\WorkSpaces\IT\GIS\Projects\SDEUpdate\CorruptTest\Corrupt.gdb gwSubBasinDwr # "AMA_CODE 'AMA_CODE' true true false 1 Text 0 0 ,First,#,Database Connections\adwrsde_9.3_login.sde\LIB.Hydrological\LIB.groundwater_subbasin_dwr,AMA_CODE,-1,-1;SUBBASIN_CODE 'SUBBASIN_CODE' true true false 2 Text 0 0 ,First,#,Database Connections\adwrsde_9.3_login.sde\LIB.Hydrological\LIB.groundwater_subbasin_dwr,SUBBASIN_CODE,-1,-1;SUBBASIN_NAME 'SUBBASIN_NAME' true true false 40 Text 0 0 ,First,#,Database Connections\adwrsde_9.3_login.sde\LIB.Hydrological\LIB.groundwater_subbasin_dwr,SUBBASIN_NAME,-1,-1;NAME_ABBR 'NAME_ABBR' true true false 3 Text 0 0 ,First,#,Database Connections\adwrsde_9.3_login.sde\LIB.Hydrological\LIB.groundwater_subbasin_dwr,NAME_ABBR,-1,-1;SHAPE_AREA 'SHAPE_AREA' false false true 0 Double 0 0 ,First,#,Database Connections\adwrsde_9.3_login.sde\LIB.Hydrological\LIB.groundwater_subbasin_dwr,SHAPE.AREA,-1,-1;SHAPE_LEN 'SHAPE_LEN' false false true 0 Double 0 0 ,First,#,Database Connections\adwrsde_9.3_login.sde\LIB.Hydrological\LIB.groundwater_subbasin_dwr,SHAPE.LEN,-1,-1" # U:\WorkSpaces\IT\GIS\Projects\SDEUpdate\CorruptTest\Corrupt.gdb\gwSubBasinDwr

FeatureClassToFeatureClass U:\WorkSpaces\IT\GIS\Projects\SDEUpdate\CorruptTest\Corrupt.gdb\gwSubBasinDwr "Database Connections\adwrsde_9.3_login.sde\LIB.Hydrological" groundwater_subbasin_dwr # "AMA_CODE 'AMA_CODE' true true false 1 Text 0 0 ,First,#,U:\WorkSpaces\IT\GIS\Projects\SDEUpdate\CorruptTest\Corrupt.gdb\gwSubBasinDwr,AMA_CODE,-1,-1;SUBBASIN_CODE 'SUBBASIN_CODE' true true false 2 Text 0 0 ,First,#,U:\WorkSpaces\IT\GIS\Projects\SDEUpdate\CorruptTest\Corrupt.gdb\gwSubBasinDwr,SUBBASIN_CODE,-1,-1;SUBBASIN_NAME 'SUBBASIN_NAME' true true false 40 Text 0 0 ,First,#,U:\WorkSpaces\IT\GIS\Projects\SDEUpdate\CorruptTest\Corrupt.gdb\gwSubBasinDwr,SUBBASIN_NAME,-1,-1;NAME_ABBR 'NAME_ABBR' true true false 3 Text 0 0 ,First,#,U:\WorkSpaces\IT\GIS\Projects\SDEUpdate\CorruptTest\Corrupt.gdb\gwSubBasinDwr,NAME_ABBR,-1,-1;Shape_Length 'Shape_Length' false true true 8 Double 0 0 ,First,#,U:\WorkSpaces\IT\GIS\Projects\SDEUpdate\CorruptTest\Corrupt.gdb\gwSubBasinDwr,Shape_Length,-1,-1;Shape_Area 'Shape_Area' false true true 8 Double 0 0 ,First,#,U:\WorkSpaces\IT\GIS\Projects\SDEUpdate\CorruptTest\Corrupt.gdb\gwSubBasinDwr,Shape_Area,-1,-1" STATIC "Database Connections\adwrsde_9.3_login.sde\LIB.Hydrological\LIB.groundwater_subbasin_dwr"

ExportFeatures LIB.groundwaterSubbasinADWR "Z:\ABOR Enhanced Recharge Project\GIS\GW_SubBasins.shp" # NOT_USE_ALIAS "AMA_CODE "AMA_CODE" true true false 1 Text 0 0,First,#,LIB.groundwaterSubbasinADWR,AMA_CODE,0,1;SUBBASIN_CODE "SUBBASIN_CODE" true true false 8 Double 8 38,First,#,LIB.groundwaterSubbasinADWR,SUBBASIN_CODE,-1,-1;SUBBASIN_NAME "SUBBASIN_NAME" true true false 40 Text 0 0,First,#,LIB.groundwaterSubbasinADWR,SUBBASIN_NAME,0,40;NAME_ABBR "NAME_ABBR" true true false 5 Text 0 0,First,#,LIB.groundwaterSubbasinADWR,NAME_ABBR,0,5;SUBBASIN_NAME_GWSI "SUBBASIN_NAME_GWSI" true true false 60 Text 0 0,First,#,LIB.groundwaterSubbasinADWR,SUBBASIN_NAME_GWSI,0,60" #

altsys.altenc: Explicit elevation coordinate included with horizontal coordinates

dataIdInfo.idAbs: According to ARS 45-402(34) "Subbasin" means an area which, as nearly as known facts permit as determined by the director pursuant to this chapter, may be designated so as to enclose a relatively hydrologically distinct body of groundwater within a groundwater basin, which shall be described horizontally by surface description.
dataIdInfo.idPurp: This feature contains groundwater Sub-basin boundaries and provide base information for use in GIS systems to aid in assessment for a variety of planning and analysis purposes and to provide a geographic view with corresponding data. These data do not represent a legal record

LegConsts.othConsts: Access is granted to public agencies. The Department makes no warranties related to the accuracy of the data and users are required to determine suitability of use for any particular purpose.

Consts.useLimit: Not for use at scales greater than 1:100000.

dataIdInfo.suppInfo: From: Kenneth C. Slowinski Sent: Wednesday, December 10, 2008 11:13 AM To: E. Frank Corkhill Cc: W. Patrick Schiffer Subject: RE: Question In the July 20, 1982 order designating groundwater basins outside of the four AMAs, the director designated 47 basins, 10 of which were divided into a total of 34 sub-basins. That order was not final because it was subject to rehearing. Rehearings were held, and a final order was issued on June 21, 1984. In the final 1984 order, the director designated 46 basins outside of the four AMAs, 9 of which were divided into a total of 27 sub-basins. In the 1984 order, the director combined the San Francisco River and Eagle Creek basins into one basin - the Morenci basin. There also were changes to several of the sub-basins. All the changes between the 1982 order and the 1984 order are described in the notebook entitled "Basin and Sub-basin Designations, June 1984." There were no changes to the Verde River basin or the sub-basins within that basin between the 1982 order and the 1984 order. However, in the 1982 order, there are findings that explain that the Verde River basin was originally proposed as three separate basins: the Mount Floyd basin, the Verde Valley basin and the Verde River basin. The three proposed basins were ultimately combined into one basin in the 1982 order (the Verde River basin), and the basin was divided into three sub-basins: the Big Chino sub-basin, the Verde Valley sub-basin and the Verde Canyon sub-basins. The 1982 findings explain why the three proposed basins were combined into one basin and also explain that the eastern boundary of the basin was changed from the approximate boundary of the C aquifer system to the Verde River surface watershed divide. If you want to see the explanations from the 1982 order, they are in the book entitled "Groundwater Basin and Sub-basin Designations, ADWR, July 1982 (pages 32-33).
